assessment.title: Helen Keller Unit - Readiness Assessment
assessment.description: Check if you're ready to learn about Helen Keller and adjectives!

questions.explanation: A noun is a naming word - it names people, places, things, or animals.

questions.questionText: Which of these is a noun?
questions.explanation: 'Book' is a noun (a thing). Run is a verb, beautiful is an adjective, quickly is an adverb.

questions.questionText: True or False: We use our ears to hear sounds.
questions.explanation: True! Ears are the organs we use for hearing sounds.
Brave means not afraid, having courage to face difficulties.

questions.questionText: Which sense do we use to see?
questions.explanation: We use the sense of sight (our eyes) to see.
Light is the opposite of dark. When there is light, there is no darkness.

questions.questionText: A word that describes something is called a/an:
questions.explanation: An adjective is a describing word. It describes nouns.

questions.questionText: True or False: Everyone can see and hear.
questions.explanation: False. Some people are blind (cannot see) or deaf (cannot hear). This doesn't stop them from achieving great things!

excellent.message: Excellent! You have strong English basics. You're ready to learn about Helen Keller and adjectives!

good.message: Good job! You have good understanding of basics. Let's learn about Helen Keller!

needsReview.message: You know some basics. Let's quickly review nouns before learning about adjectives.

needsPrep.message: Let's strengthen your understanding of nouns first. Then adjectives will be easier to learn!
